cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est posée de manière générale, sans faits contractuels détaillés ; l’analyse suppose donc un contrat relevant d’IFRS 15 portant sur une licence de propriété intellectuelle accordée à un client.\\\",\\n    \\\"L’analyse vise d’abord le cas où la licence constitue une obligation de prestation distincte ; si elle n’est pas distincte, son rythme de constatation suit l’obligation de prestation unique correspondante.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Sous IFRS 15, le revenu de licence peut être constaté soit sur la durée, soit à une date donnée selon la nature de la promesse. Si la licence donne un droit d’accès à la PI, le revenu est étalé ; si elle donne un droit d’utilisation de la PI existant à l’octroi, le revenu est constaté à un moment donné.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_access_the_entitys_intellectual_property\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’accès à la propriété intellectuelle de l’entité\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Ce traitement s’applique dans cette situation seulement si la promesse de licence est de fournir un accès continu à la PI pendant la durée de la licence. IFRS 15 B58 exige que l’entité mène des activités affectant significativement la PI et que le client soit exposé à leurs effets ; dans ce cas, IFRS 15 B60 impose une reconnaissance du revenu au fil du temps, car le client reçoit et consomme simultanément le bénéfice de l’accès (IFRS 15 35(a)).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Le contrat exige, ou le client s’attend raisonnablement à, des activités de l’entité qui affectent significativement la propriété intellectuelle.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les droits concédés exposent directement le client aux effets positifs ou négatifs de ces activités.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ces activités ne transfèrent pas au client un bien ou un service distinct au fur et à mesure.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu est constaté de façon étalée sur la période de la licence, selon une méthode de mesure de l’avancement conforme à IFRS 15 39–45.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B58\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The nature of an entity’s promise in granting a licence is a promise to provide a right to access the entity’s intellectual property if **all of the following criteria are met**: **(a)the contract requires, or the customer reasonably expects, that the entity will undertake activities that significantly affect the intellectual property** to which the customer has rights (see paragraphs B59 and B59A); **(b)the rights granted by the licence directly expose the customer to any positive or negative effects of the entity’s activities** identified in paragraph B58(a) ; and **(c)those activities do not result in the transfer of a good or a service to the customer as those activities occur** (see paragraph 25).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B60\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are met, an entity shall account for the promise to grant a licence as a **performance obligation satisfied over time** because the customer will **simultaneously receive and consume the benefit** from the entity’s performance of providing access to its intellectual property as the performance occurs (see paragraph 35(a)). An entity shall apply paragraphs 39⁠–⁠45 to select an appropriate method to measure its progress towards complete satisfaction of that performance obligation to provide access.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity transfers control of a good or service over time and, therefore, satisfies a performance obligation and recognises revenue over time, if one of the following criteria is met: E4, E5 **(a)the customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits provided by the entity’s performance as the entity performs** (see paragraphs B3⁠–⁠B4); (b)the entity’s performance creates or enhances an asset (for example, work in progress) that the customer controls as the asset is created or enhanced (see paragraph B5); or (c)the entity’s performance does not create an asset with an alternative use to the entity (see paragraph 36) and the entity has an enforceable right to payment for performance completed to date (see paragraph 37). E6\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_use_the_entitys_intellectual_property\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’utilisation de la propriété intellectuelle de l’entité\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Ce traitement s’applique dans cette situation si les critères du droit d’accès ne sont pas remplis et que la licence donne au client le droit d’utiliser la PI telle qu’elle existe à la date d’octroi. IFRS 15 B61 précise alors que l’obligation est satisfaite à une date donnée ; le point de transfert se détermine selon les indicateurs de contrôle d’IFRS 15 38, sans reconnaître de revenu avant que le client puisse effectivement utiliser et tirer bénéfice de la licence.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Les critères du paragraphe IFRS 15 B58 ne sont pas remplis.\\\",\\n        \\\"La licence donne au client un droit d’utiliser la propriété intellectuelle telle qu’elle existe en forme et fonctionnalité à la date d’octroi.\\\",\\n        \\\"La période d’utilisation a commencé et la licence a été mise à disposition du client pour usage immédiat.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu est constaté à la date de transfert du contrôle de la licence, sous réserve que le client puisse effectivement l’utiliser.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B61\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are not met, the nature of an entity’s promise is to provide a **right to use the entity’s intellectual property as that intellectual property exists** (in terms of form and functionality) at the point in time at which the licence is granted to the customer. This means that the customer can direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the licence **at the point in time at which the licence transfers**. An entity shall account for the promise to provide a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as a **performance obligation satisfied at a point in time**. An entity shall apply paragraph 38 to determine the point in time at which the licence transfers to the customer. However, revenue cannot be recognised for a licence that provides a right to use the entity’s intellectual property **before the beginning of the period during which the customer is able to use and benefit from the licence**. For example, if a software licence period begins before an entity provides (or otherwise makes available) to the customer a code that enables the customer to immediately use the software, the entity would not recognise revenue before that code has been provided (or otherwise made available).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"38\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a performance obligation is not satisfied over time in accordance with paragraphs 35⁠–⁠37, an entity satisfies the obligation **at a point in time**. To determine the point in time at which a customer obtains control of a promised asset and the entity satisfies a performance obligation, the entity shall consider the requirements for control in paragraphs 31⁠–⁠34. In addition, an entity shall consider indicators of the transfer of control, which include, but are not limited to, the following: **(a)The entity has a present right to payment for the asset**—if a customer is presently obliged to pay for an asset, then that may indicate that the customer has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset in exchange. **(b)The customer has legal title to the asset**—legal title may indicate which party to a contract has the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, an asset or to restrict the access of other entities to those benefits. Therefore, the transfer of legal title of an asset may indicate that the customer has obtained control of the asset. If an entity retains legal title solely as protection against the customer’s failure to pay, those rights of the entity would not preclude the customer from obtaining control of an asset. **(c)The entity has transferred physical possession of the asset**—the customer’s physical possession of an asset may indicate that the customer has the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset or to restrict the access of other entities to those benefits. However, physical possession may not coincide with control of an asset. For example, in some repurchase agreements and in some consignment arrangements, a customer or consignee may have physical possession of an asset that the entity controls. Conversely, in some bill-and-hold arrangements, the entity may have physical possession of an asset that the customer controls. Paragraphs B64⁠–⁠B76, B77⁠–⁠B78 and B79⁠–⁠B82 provide guidance on accounting for repurchase agreements, consignment arrangements and bill-and-hold arrangements, respectively. **(d)The customer has the significant risks and rewards of ownership of the asset**—the transfer of the significant risks and rewards of ownership of an asset to the customer may indicate that the customer has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset. However, when evaluating the risks and rewards of ownership of a promised asset, an entity shall exclude any risks that give rise to a separate performance obligation in addition to the performance obligation to transfer the asset. For example, an entity may have transferred control of an asset to a customer but not yet satisfied an additional performance obligation to provide maintenance services related to the transferred asset. **(e)The customer has accepted the asset**—the customer’s acceptance of an asset may indicate that it has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset. To evaluate the effect of a contractual customer acceptance clause on when control of an asset is transferred, an entity shall consider the guidance in paragraphs B83⁠–⁠B86.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778862622929}

cache: keyv:repeat:1:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:55f3757ef2440137105617a37a6438de62a2fd0aa11582cfee8187494c95a943:Comment constater le revenu lié aux licences de propriété intellectuelle : à une date donnée ou de façon étalée sur la durée du droit ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/53_initial_eval-retrieve_for_Q9.0/runs/2026-05-01_16-28-59_promptfoo-eval-family-q9"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est posée de manière générale et ne précise pas si la licence est distincte des autres biens ou services du contrat.\\\",\\n    \\\"Aucun fait ne permet de conclure définitivement si la promesse porte sur un droit d’accès à la propriété intellectuelle, un droit d’utilisation, ou une obligation combinée.\\\",\\n    \\\"Le contrat est supposé relever d’IFRS 15 et concerner une licence de propriété intellectuelle au sens d’IFRS 15.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Sous IFRS 15, le revenu d’une licence de propriété intellectuelle peut être constaté soit sur la durée, soit à une date donnée. Le traitement dépend d’abord du caractère distinct ou non de la licence, puis, si elle est distincte, de la nature du droit concédé : droit d’accès ou droit d’utilisation.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_access_intellectual_property\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’accès à la propriété intellectuelle\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette méthode s’applique dans cette situation seulement si la licence est distincte et si la promesse consiste à donner accès à la propriété intellectuelle pendant la période de licence, et non à en transférer l’usage figé à l’origine (IFRS 15 B56, IFRS 15 B58). Dans ce cas, IFRS 15 impose une comptabilisation sur la durée, car le client reçoit et consomme le bénéfice au fur et à mesure de la performance (IFRS 15 B60, IFRS 15 35(a)).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La licence est distincte des autres biens ou services du contrat.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le contrat exige, ou le client s’attend raisonnablement à, des activités de l’entité qui affectent significativement la propriété intellectuelle pendant la période de licence.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu est reconnu étalé sur la durée de la licence selon une méthode de mesure de l’avancement appropriée.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the promise to grant the licence is distinct from the other promised goods or services in the contract and, therefore, the promise to grant the licence is a separate performance obligation, an entity shall determine whether the licence transfers to a customer either at a point in time or over time. In making this determination, an entity shall consider whether the nature of the entity’s promise in granting the licence to a customer is to provide the customer with either: **(a)a right to access the entity’s intellectual property as it exists throughout the licence period;** or **(b)a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as it exists at the point in time at which the licence is granted.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B58\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The nature of an entity’s promise in granting a licence is a promise to provide a right to access the entity’s intellectual property if **all of the following criteria are met:** **(a)the contract requires, or the customer reasonably expects, that the entity will undertake activities that significantly affect the intellectual property to which the customer has rights** (see paragraphs B59 and B59A); **(b)the rights granted by the licence directly expose the customer to any positive or negative effects of the entity’s activities identified in paragraph B58(a)** ; and **(c)those activities do not result in the transfer of a good or a service to the customer as those activities occur** (see paragraph 25).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B60\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are met, an entity shall account for the promise to grant a licence as **a performance obligation satisfied over time** because the customer will **simultaneously receive and consume the benefit from the entity’s performance of providing access to its intellectual property as the performance occurs** (see paragraph 35(a)). An entity shall apply paragraphs 39⁠–⁠45 to select an appropriate method to measure its progress towards complete satisfaction of that performance obligation to provide access.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity transfers control of a good or service over time and, therefore, satisfies a performance obligation and recognises revenue over time, if one of the following criteria is met: E4, E5 **(a)the customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits provided by the entity’s performance as the entity performs** (see paragraphs B3⁠–⁠B4); (b)the entity’s performance creates or enhances an asset (for example, work in progress) that the customer controls as the asset is created or enhanced (see paragraph B5); or (c)the entity’s performance does not create an asset with an alternative use to the entity (see paragraph 36) and the entity has an enforceable right to payment for performance completed to date (see paragraph 37). E6\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_use_intellectual_property\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’utilisation de la propriété intellectuelle\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette méthode s’applique dans cette situation si la licence est distincte mais que les critères du droit d’accès ne sont pas remplis; la promesse est alors un droit d’utiliser la propriété intellectuelle telle qu’elle existe à la date d’octroi (IFRS 15 B56, IFRS 15 B61). Le revenu est alors comptabilisé à une date donnée, au moment du transfert du contrôle, sans reconnaissance avant que le client puisse utiliser et tirer avantage de la licence (IFRS 15 B61, IFRS 15 38).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La licence est distincte des autres biens ou services du contrat.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les critères d’IFRS 15 B58 relatifs au droit d’accès ne sont pas remplis.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu est reconnu à la date de transfert de la licence, au plus tôt lorsque le client peut effectivement l’utiliser.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the promise to grant the licence is distinct from the other promised goods or services in the contract and, therefore, the promise to grant the licence is a separate performance obligation, an entity shall determine whether the licence transfers to a customer either at a point in time or over time. In making this determination, an entity shall consider whether the nature of the entity’s promise in granting the licence to a customer is to provide the customer with either: (a)a right to access the entity’s intellectual property as it exists throughout the licence period; or **(b)a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as it exists at the point in time at which the licence is granted.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B61\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are not met, the nature of an entity’s promise is to provide **a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as that intellectual property exists (in terms of form and functionality) at the point in time at which the licence is granted to the customer.** This means that the customer can direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the licence at the point in time at which the licence transfers. **An entity shall account for the promise to provide a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as a performance obligation satisfied at a point in time.** An entity shall apply paragraph 38 to determine the point in time at which the licence transfers to the customer. **However, revenue cannot be recognised for a licence that provides a right to use the entity’s intellectual property before the beginning of the period during which the customer is able to use and benefit from the licence.** For example, if a software licence period begins before an entity provides (or otherwise makes available) to the customer a code that enables the customer to immediately use the software, the entity would not recognise revenue before that code has been provided (or otherwise made available).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"38\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a performance obligation is not satisfied over time in accordance with paragraphs 35⁠–⁠37, an entity satisfies the performance obligation **at a point in time.** To determine the point in time at which a customer obtains control of a promised asset and the entity satisfies a performance obligation, the entity shall consider the requirements for control in paragraphs 31⁠–⁠34. In addition, an entity shall consider indicators of the transfer of control, which include, but are not limited to, the following: (a)The entity has a present right to payment for the asset—if a customer is presently obliged to pay for an asset, then that may indicate that the customer has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset in exchange. (b)The customer has legal title to the asset—legal title may indicate which party to a contract has the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, an asset or to restrict the access of other entities to those benefits. Therefore, the transfer of legal title of an asset may indicate that the customer has obtained control of the asset. If an entity retains legal title solely as protection against the customer’s failure to pay, those rights of the entity would not preclude the customer from obtaining control of an asset. (c)The entity has transferred physical possession of the asset—the customer’s physical possession of an asset may indicate that the customer has the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset or to restrict the access of other entities to those benefits. However, physical possession may not coincide with control of an asset. For example, in some repurchase agreements and in some consignment arrangements, a customer or consignee may have physical possession of an asset that the entity controls. Conversely, in some bill-and-hold arrangements, the entity may have physical possession of an asset that the customer controls. Paragraphs B64⁠–⁠B76, B77⁠–⁠B78 and B79⁠–⁠B82 provide guidance on accounting for repurchase agreements, consignment arrangements and bill-and-hold arrangements, respectively. (d)The customer has the significant risks and rewards of ownership of the asset—the transfer of the significant risks and rewards of ownership of an asset to the customer may indicate that the customer has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset. However, when evaluating the risks and rewards of ownership of a promised asset, an entity shall exclude any risks that give rise to a separate performance obligation in addition to the performance obligation to transfer the asset. For example, an entity may have transferred control of an asset to a customer but not yet satisfied an additional performance obligation to provide maintenance services related to the transferred asset. (e)The customer has accepted the asset—the customer’s acceptance of an asset may indicate that it has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset. To evaluate the effect of a contractual customer acceptance clause on when control of an asset is transferred, an entity shall consider the guidance in paragraphs B83⁠–⁠B86.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_3\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"revenue_recognised_over_time\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Licence non distincte : revenu reconnu sur la durée\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette méthode ne s’applique dans cette situation que si la licence n’est pas distincte et doit être combinée avec d’autres biens ou services en une seule obligation de prestation (IFRS 15 B54, IFRS 15 B55). Le revenu est alors reconnu sur la durée uniquement si l’obligation combinée satisfait l’un des critères généraux de transfert du contrôle au fil du temps prévus par IFRS 15 35 (IFRS 15 32, IFRS 15 35).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La licence n’est pas distincte des autres biens ou services du contrat.\\\",\\n        \\\"L’obligation de prestation combinée remplit au moins un des critères de reconnaissance du revenu sur la durée.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu est reconnu selon l’avancement de l’obligation combinée, et non selon les seules règles spécifiques aux licences distinctes.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B54\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the promise to grant a licence is not distinct from other promised goods or services in the contract in accordance with paragraphs 26⁠–⁠30, an entity shall account for **the promise to grant a licence and those other promised goods or services together as a single performance obligation.** Examples of licences that are not distinct from other goods or services promised in the contract include the following: (a)a licence that forms a component of a tangible good and that is integral to the functionality of the good; and (b)a licence that the customer can benefit from only in conjunction with a related service (such as an online service provided by the entity that enables, by granting a licence, the customer to access content).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B55\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the licence is not distinct, an entity shall apply paragraphs 31⁠–⁠38 to determine whether **the performance obligation (which includes the promised licence) is a performance obligation that is satisfied over time** or satisfied at a point in time.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"32\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For each performance obligation identified in accordance with paragraphs 22⁠–⁠30, an entity shall determine at contract inception whether it satisfies the performance obligation **over time** (in accordance with paragraphs 35⁠–⁠37) or satisfies the performance obligation **at a point in time** (in accordance with paragraph 38). If an entity does not satisfy a performance obligation over time, the performance obligation is satisfied at a point in time.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity transfers control of a good or service over time and, therefore, satisfies a performance obligation and recognises revenue over time, if one of the following criteria is met: E4, E5 **(a)the customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits provided by the entity’s performance as the entity performs** (see paragraphs B3⁠–⁠B4); **(b)the entity’s performance creates or enhances an asset (for example, work in progress) that the customer controls as the asset is created or enhanced** (see paragraph B5); or **(c)the entity’s performance does not create an asset with an alternative use to the entity** (see paragraph 36) **and the entity has an enforceable right to payment for performance completed to date** (see paragraph 37). E6\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_4\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"revenue_recognised_at_a_point_in_time\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Licence non distincte : revenu reconnu à une date donnée\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette méthode s’applique dans cette situation si la licence n’est pas distincte, qu’elle est intégrée à une obligation de prestation unique, et que cette obligation ne remplit pas les critères de reconnaissance sur la durée (IFRS 15 B55, IFRS 15 32). Dans ce cas, IFRS 15 impose une reconnaissance à une date donnée, déterminée par le transfert du contrôle selon les indicateurs du paragraphe 38 (IFRS 15 38).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La licence n’est pas distincte des autres biens ou services du contrat.\\\",\\n        \\\"L’obligation de prestation combinée ne remplit aucun des critères de reconnaissance du revenu sur la durée.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu est reconnu lorsque le contrôle de l’obligation combinée est transféré au client.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B55\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the licence is not distinct, an entity shall apply paragraphs 31⁠–⁠38 to determine whether the performance obligation (which includes the promised licence) is a performance obligation that is **satisfied over time or satisfied at a point in time.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"32\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For each performance obligation identified in accordance with paragraphs 22⁠–⁠30, an entity shall determine at contract inception whether it satisfies the performance obligation **over time** (in accordance with paragraphs 35⁠–⁠37) or satisfies the performance obligation **at a point in time** (in accordance with paragraph 38). **If an entity does not satisfy a performance obligation over time, the performance obligation is satisfied at a point in time.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"38\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a performance obligation is not satisfied over time in accordance with paragraphs 35⁠–⁠37, an entity satisfies the performance obligation **at a point in time.** To determine the point in time at which a customer obtains control of a promised asset and the entity satisfies a performance obligation, the entity shall consider the requirements for control in paragraphs 31⁠–⁠34. In addition, an entity shall consider indicators of the transfer of control, which include, but are not limited to, the following: (a)The entity has a present right to payment for the asset—if a customer is presently obliged to pay for an asset, then that may indicate that the customer has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset in exchange. (b)The customer has legal title to the asset—legal title may indicate which party to a contract has the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, an asset or to restrict the access of other entities to those benefits. Therefore, the transfer of legal title of an asset may indicate that the customer has obtained control of the asset. If an entity retains legal title solely as protection against the customer’s failure to pay, those rights of the entity would not preclude the customer from obtaining control of an asset. (c)The entity has transferred physical possession of the asset—the customer’s physical possession of an asset may indicate that the customer has the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset or to restrict the access of other entities to those benefits. However, physical possession may not coincide with control of an asset. For example, in some repurchase agreements and in some consignment arrangements, a customer or consignee may have physical possession of an asset that the entity controls. Conversely, in some bill-and-hold arrangements, the entity may have physical possession of an asset that the customer controls. Paragraphs B64⁠–⁠B76, B77⁠–⁠B78 and B79⁠–⁠B82 provide guidance on accounting for repurchase agreements, consignment arrangements and bill-and-hold arrangements, respectively. (d)The customer has the significant risks and rewards of ownership of the asset—the transfer of the significant risks and rewards of ownership of an asset to the customer may indicate that the customer has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset. However, when evaluating the risks and rewards of ownership of a promised asset, an entity shall exclude any risks that give rise to a separate performance obligation in addition to the performance obligation to transfer the asset. For example, an entity may have transferred control of an asset to a customer but not yet satisfied an additional performance obligation to provide maintenance services related to the transferred asset. (e)The customer has accepted the asset—the customer’s acceptance of an asset may indicate that it has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset. To evaluate the effect of a contractual customer acceptance clause on when control of an asset is transferred, an entity shall consider the guidance in paragraphs B83⁠–⁠B86.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778862661903}

cache: keyv:repeat:1:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:55f3757ef2440137105617a37a6438de62a2fd0aa11582cfee8187494c95a943:Comment constater le revenu lié aux licences de propriété intellectuelle : à une date donnée ou de façon étalée sur la durée du droit ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/53_initial_eval-retrieve_for_Q9.0/runs/2026-05-01_16-54-11_promptfoo-eval-family-q9"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est posée de manière générale et ne fournit pas de faits contractuels permettant de conclure si la licence confère un droit d’accès ou un droit d’utilisation.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que l’analyse est faite dans le champ d’IFRS 15 pour une licence de propriété intellectuelle accordée à un client.\\\",\\n    \\\"Si la licence n’est pas distincte d’autres biens ou services, le calendrier de comptabilisation se juge au niveau de l’obligation de prestation unique incluant la licence.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Sous IFRS 15, le revenu d’une licence de propriété intellectuelle peut être comptabilisé soit étalé dans le temps, soit à une date donnée. Le choix dépend de la nature de la promesse : droit d’accès si les critères d’IFRS 15 B58 sont remplis, sinon droit d’utilisation selon IFRS 15 B61.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_access_intellectual_property\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’accès à la propriété intellectuelle\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette méthode s’applique dans cette situation seulement si la licence donne accès à la propriété intellectuelle pendant toute la durée du droit, ce qui exige que les trois critères d’IFRS 15 B58 soient réunis. Si tel est le cas, IFRS 15 B60 impose une satisfaction de l’obligation de prestation dans le temps, car le client reçoit et consomme simultanément le bénéfice de l’accès au fur et à mesure.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Le contrat impose, ou le client s’attend raisonnablement à, des activités de l’entité qui affectent significativement la propriété intellectuelle.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les droits accordés exposent directement le client aux effets positifs ou négatifs de ces activités.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ces activités ne donnent pas lieu au transfert d’un bien ou service distinct au fur et à mesure.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu est reconnu de façon étalée sur la période de licence selon une méthode de mesure de l’avancement appropriée.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B58\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The nature of an entity’s promise in granting a licence is a promise to provide a right to access the entity’s intellectual property **if all of the following criteria are met**: **(a) the contract requires, or the customer reasonably expects, that the entity will undertake activities that significantly affect the intellectual property** to which the customer has rights (see paragraphs B59 and B59A); **(b) the rights granted by the licence directly expose the customer to any positive or negative effects of the entity’s activities** identified in paragraph B58(a) ; and **(c) those activities do not result in the transfer of a good or a service to the customer as those activities occur** (see paragraph 25).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B60\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are met, an entity shall account for the promise to grant a licence **as a performance obligation satisfied over time** because **the customer will simultaneously receive and consume the benefit from the entity’s performance of providing access to its intellectual property as the performance occurs** (see paragraph 35(a)). An entity shall apply paragraphs 39⁠–⁠45 to select an appropriate method to measure its progress towards complete satisfaction of that performance obligation to provide access.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_use_intellectual_property\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’utilisation de la propriété intellectuelle\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette méthode s’applique dans cette situation si les critères du droit d’accès ne sont pas remplis. Dans ce cas, IFRS 15 B61 qualifie la promesse de droit d’utilisation de la propriété intellectuelle telle qu’elle existe à la date d’octroi, et le revenu est alors comptabilisé à un moment donné, en appliquant les indicateurs de transfert du contrôle d’IFRS 15 38.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Les critères d’IFRS 15 B58 pour un droit d’accès ne sont pas remplis.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le client peut utiliser et tirer profit de la licence à compter du moment où celle-ci est transférée ou rendue disponible.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu est reconnu à la date de transfert de la licence, sans reconnaissance avant le début de la période d’utilisation possible.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B61\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are not met, the nature of an entity’s promise is to provide **a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as that intellectual property exists (in terms of form and functionality) at the point in time at which the licence is granted to the customer**. This means that **the customer can direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the licence at the point in time at which the licence transfers**. An entity shall account for the promise to provide a right to use the entity’s intellectual property **as a performance obligation satisfied at a point in time**. An entity shall apply paragraph 38 to determine the point in time at which the licence transfers to the customer. However, revenue cannot be recognised for a licence that provides a right to use the entity’s intellectual property before the beginning of the period during which the customer is able to use and benefit from the licence. For example, if a software licence period begins before an entity provides (or otherwise makes available) to the customer a code that enables the customer to immediately use the software, the entity would not recognise revenue before that code has been provided (or otherwise made available).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"38\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a performance obligation is not satisfied over time in accordance with paragraphs 35⁠–⁠37, an entity **satisfies the performance obligation at a point in time**. To determine the point in time at which a customer obtains control of a promised asset and the entity satisfies a performance obligation, the entity shall consider the requirements for control in paragraphs 31⁠–⁠34. In addition, an entity shall consider indicators of the transfer of control, which include, but are not limited to, the following: **(a) The entity has a present right to payment for the asset**—if a customer is presently obliged to pay for an asset, then that may indicate that the customer has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset in exchange. **(b) The customer has legal title to the asset**—legal title may indicate which party to a contract has the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, an asset or to restrict the access of other entities to those benefits. Therefore, the transfer of legal title of an asset may indicate that the customer has obtained control of the asset. If an entity retains legal title solely as protection against the customer’s failure to pay, those rights of the entity would not preclude the customer from obtaining control of an asset. **(c) The entity has transferred physical possession of the asset**—the customer’s physical possession of an asset may indicate that the customer has the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset or to restrict the access of other entities to those benefits. However, physical possession may not coincide with control of an asset. For example, in some repurchase agreements and in some consignment arrangements, a customer or consignee may have physical possession of an asset that the entity controls. Conversely, in some bill-and-hold arrangements, the entity may have physical possession of an asset that the customer controls. Paragraphs B64⁠–⁠B76, B77⁠–⁠B78 and B79⁠–⁠B82 provide guidance on accounting for repurchase agreements, consignment arrangements and bill-and-hold arrangements, respectively. **(d) The customer has the significant risks and rewards of ownership of the asset**—the transfer of the significant risks and rewards of ownership of an asset to the customer may indicate that the customer has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset. However, when evaluating the risks and rewards of ownership of a promised asset, an entity shall exclude any risks that give rise to a separate performance obligation in addition to the performance obligation to transfer the asset. For example, an entity may have transferred control of an asset to a customer but not yet satisfied an additional performance obligation to provide maintenance services related to the transferred asset. **(e) The customer has accepted the asset**—the customer’s acceptance of an asset may indicate that it has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset. To evaluate the effect of a contractual customer acceptance clause on when control of an asset is transferred, an entity shall consider the guidance in paragraphs B83⁠–⁠B86.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778864128884}
